signal: Companies House filing: change of registered office — second Deansgate location added (Jan 2026).
m1_line: I noticed Two Pines opened a second shop on Deansgate in January — who's currently approving expense reports across both stores, and how long does it take?
buyer_reply: We already use our high-street bank's corporate Visa — why switch?
m2_line: So you've got the card-issuing side sorted with the high-street bank — what's not working about how you see spend across the two stores?
prop_pitch: Per-store virtual cards with auto-reconciled receipts — your bookkeeper sees both shops in one view.
m3_line: Two 20-minute slots: Tuesday 10:00 or Wednesday 10:30 — which works to walk through it with your bookkeeper?

signal: LinkedIn: announced $18M Series B (Apr 2026). Hiring 6 engineers + Head of Finance.
m1_line: Saw your Series-B announcement last month — congrats. How are you handling AWS spend visibility now that engineering has more autonomy?
buyer_reply: We're already on Brex — why would we switch?
m2_line: Brex covers the issuing side well in the US — what does FX cost you on your EU SaaS subscriptions today?
prop_pitch: Real-time per-team budgets with hard limits + interbank FX — additive to Brex, not a replacement.
m3_line: Thursday 16:00 or Friday 09:30 — happy to walk you and your head of finance through a side-by-side with your current Brex setup.

signal: Companies House: annual accounts filed — turnover up 22% YoY. Hiring shift supervisor.
m1_line: I saw Brightline's latest filing — looks like you've grown headcount 18% this year. How much time does your bookkeeping team spend re-keying supplier receipts each month?
buyer_reply: Our accountant won't deal with another tool — last vendor said Sage sync worked but it broke.
m2_line: So the last Sage integration broke and that's the trust question — what specifically failed? Was it the line-level export or the reconciliation?
prop_pitch: Per-job-number cards with Sage line-level export and supervisor approval — and a 10-min call with your accountant to walk the sync.
m3_line: Could I get 20 minutes with you and your Sage admin Tuesday at 14:00, or Thursday at 11:30?

signal: Handelsregister-Eintrag for 4th location in Schwabing (Feb 2026). LinkedIn: 'four locations, one menu' press feature.
m1_line: Tafelrunde hat im letzten Quartal die vierte Filiale in Schwabing eröffnet — wie regelt ihr im Moment die Kartenausgaben über die vier Standorte hinweg?
buyer_reply: Ich vertraue neuen Fintechs nicht mit meinem Geld — und GDPR, wo liegen die Daten?
m2_line: Die Vertrauensfrage ist fair — Gelder werden bei einer regulierten Tier-1-Partnerbank gehalten, Daten in der EU. Was wäre für dich der konkrete Beweis, dass es seriös ist?
prop_pitch: Per-location Karten mit Echtzeit-Limits + EU-Datensitz + Auto-FX für italienische Lieferanten.
m3_line: 20 Minuten mit dir und deinem zentralen Buchhalter — Donnerstag 10:00 oder Freitag 09:30? Du fragst alle Vertrauensfragen, dann entscheidest du.
